evals.prompt: I just downloaded a service account JSON key from Google Cloud Console for our Android app com.acme.todo. I need to set up gpc so I can start using it to manage releases. The key file is at ~/Downloads/play-api-key.json. Can you walk me through the whole setup?
evals.expected_output: GPC is installed, authenticated with the service account, configured with the app package name, and gpc doctor passes all checks
expectations: Installs GPC via npm install -g gpc or suggests npx
Runs gpc auth login --service-account with the key path
Sets the default app to com.acme.todo via gpc config or .gpcrc.json
Runs gpc doctor to verify the setup
Mentions GPC_SERVICE_ACCOUNT env var as CI alternative

evals.prompt: gpc auth status is showing AUTH_EXPIRED and I can't figure out why. I'm using a service account but it was working fine yesterday. We're behind a corporate proxy at proxy.internal.corp:8080 and I think they recently changed the SSL cert.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the likely cause as proxy/SSL issue, provides fix with HTTPS_PROXY and GPC_CA_CERT environment variables, and re-auth steps
expectations: Identifies proxy/SSL as likely cause given the context
Mentions GPC_CA_CERT for custom CA certificate
Suggests re-running gpc auth login after fixing network config
Recommends gpc doctor to verify connectivity

evals.prompt: We have 3 different Google Play developer accounts — one for our main app, one for a white-label client, and a sandbox account for testing. How do I switch between them without re-authenticating every time?
evals.expected_output: Explains GPC auth profiles for managing multiple accounts, shows how to set up and switch between them
expectations: Shows how to create named profiles with different service accounts
Shows gpc auth switch for switching between profiles
